title: Foundations, logic and type theory
summary: Logical foundations, dependent type theory, proof terms and the formal substrate used by Lean.

targets: Map graph node semantics to Lean structures and metadata.

questions: Which informal mathematical structures need explicit universe or typeclass control?

title: Category theory
summary: Language of objects, morphisms, functors, natural transformations, adjunctions and universal properties.

targets: Use functoriality as a formal bridge pattern.

questions: Can a proposed analogy be upgraded into a functor, adjunction or equivalence?

title: Algebra
summary: Groups, rings, modules, fields, algebras and their structure-preserving maps.

targets: Connect algebraic structure nodes to mathlib typeclass hierarchies.

questions: Which bridge requires structure, representation or invariant theory?

title: Topology
summary: Continuity, compactness, connectedness, homotopy and global qualitative structure.

targets: Track compactness and continuity assumptions in cross-domain arguments.

questions: When does a local construction fail to glue globally?

title: Analysis
summary: Limits, differentiability, integration, measure and analytic estimates.

targets: Create toy estimates that can be reused by PDE and spectral nodes.

questions: Which estimate is the actual bottleneck of a proposed proof route?

title: Functional analysis
summary: Normed spaces, Banach and Hilbert spaces, operators and weak convergence.

targets: Formalize operator-theoretic toy bridges.

questions: Can a nonlinear problem be embedded into a linear or spectral setting?

title: Harmonic analysis
summary: Fourier analysis, singular integrals, oscillation and scale-sensitive decomposition.

targets: Identify available Fourier transform infrastructure for toy estimates.

questions: Which hidden frequency interaction blocks a global estimate?

title: Spectral theory
summary: Eigenvalues, spectra of operators, spectral gaps and trace information.

targets: State finite-dimensional spectral gap toy problems.

questions: Can a zero, obstruction or phase transition be reinterpreted as spectral data?

title: Probability and stochastic processes
summary: Random variables, stochastic dynamics, concentration and probabilistic method.

targets: Build finite probabilistic toy models for analytic or complexity barriers.

questions: Does randomness reveal the typical obstruction or only average it away?

title: Partial differential equations
summary: Equations involving functions and derivatives across space, time and boundary data.

targets: Represent weak and strong toy PDE statements with explicit assumptions.

questions: Where does regularity transfer fail: estimates, compactness or uniqueness?

title: Differential geometry
summary: Manifolds, tangent structures, connections, curvature and geometric flows.

targets: Catalog which manifold prerequisites are already formalized.

questions: Can an analytic obstruction be rephrased as curvature or geometry?

title: Symplectic geometry
summary: Hamiltonian geometry, phase space, symplectic forms and quantization shadows.

targets: Locate and extend formal symplectic primitives.

questions: Does a bridge preserve the phase-space structure or only the equations?

title: Algebraic geometry
summary: Varieties, schemes, sheaves, cohomology and geometric encoding of algebraic data.

targets: Connect geometric objects to cohomological bridge nodes.

questions: Which algebraic statement becomes simpler after adding geometry?

title: Arithmetic geometry
summary: Number theory through geometric objects over fields, rings and schemes.

targets: Map elliptic curve and L-function prerequisites.

questions: Can analytic rank information be represented as geometric or cohomological data?

title: Number theory
summary: Integers, primes, zeta functions, arithmetic functions and Diophantine structure.

targets: Formalize finite Euler product and explicit-formula toy models.

questions: Which arithmetic object has a spectral, geometric or probabilistic avatar?

title: Automorphic forms
summary: Symmetries, representations and analytic functions connecting geometry and arithmetic.

targets: Find formal islands around modular forms and characters.

questions: Can a number-theoretic question be moved into representation theory?

title: Representation theory
summary: Study of algebraic structures through their actions on spaces.

targets: Use actions as a bridge between algebra, geometry and physics.

questions: What symmetry is invisible in the original formulation?

title: Quantum field theory
summary: Fields, quantization, path integrals, operator algebras and renormalization heuristics.

targets: Replace informal path-integral claims with finite or algebraic toy models.

questions: Which physical heuristic has a finite, constructive or operator-algebraic version?

title: Gauge theory
summary: Connections, curvature, principal bundles, gauge symmetry and Yang-Mills functionals.

targets: Create finite gauge-action models before continuous field theories.

questions: Can a gauge-invariant observable expose a spectral gap?

title: General relativity
summary: Lorentzian geometry, curvature, Einstein equations and geometric analysis.

targets: Track formal prerequisites for semi-Riemannian toy geometry.

questions: Which geometric PDE tools transfer between relativity and other flows?

title: Complexity theory
summary: Resources needed for computation, reductions, circuits, proof systems and barriers.

targets: Formalize finite languages, reductions and toy circuit classes.

questions: Which lower-bound barrier is structural rather than technical?

title: Noncommutative geometry
summary: Geometric reasoning where coordinate algebras need not commute.

targets: Find operator algebra primitives for spectral triple toy models.

questions: Is a missing geometric space better represented by a noncommutative algebra?

title: Optimal transport
summary: Geometry of probability measures, transport cost, Wasserstein spaces and curvature-like flow ideas.

targets: Build finite transport problems as formal toy models.

questions: Can measure geometry reveal monotonicity or compactness missing in PDE?

title: Trace formula as spectral arithmetic bridge
summary: Relates spectral data, orbital/geometric data and arithmetic counting through trace identities.

targets: Formalize finite trace identities before analytic trace formulas.

questions: Which toy trace formula preserves enough structure to test RH-style analogies?

title: Scaling and renormalization for critical PDE
summary: Uses scaling, compactness and energy flow across scales to compare PDE and field-theoretic phenomena.

targets: State a toy scale-invariant norm estimate.

questions: Can the critical obstruction be isolated as a monotone quantity or fixed point?

title: Motives, periods and Hodge structures
summary: Connects algebraic cycles, cohomology, periods and arithmetic shadows.

targets: Represent a tiny cycle/cohomology dictionary as data.

questions: Which conjectural bridge can be tested on low-dimensional varieties?

title: Geometric Langlands transfer
summary: Transforms arithmetic and representation-theoretic questions into geometric categorical language.

targets: Use finite categorical analogues to model transfer.

questions: Which part of a problem becomes functorial after geometric translation?

title: Random matrices, zeta zeros and spectra
summary: Compares statistical behavior of zeta zeros with eigenvalue statistics from random matrix ensembles.

targets: Formalize finite eigenvalue-statistic toy examples.

questions: What part of the analogy is structural, and what part is only statistical?

title: Proof complexity and geometry
summary: Studies computational lower bounds through algebraic, geometric and proof-system obstructions.

targets: Formalize tiny proof systems and reductions.

questions: Can a barrier be represented as an invariant that survives reductions?

title: Riemann Hypothesis
summary: Nontrivial zeros of the Riemann zeta function are expected to lie on the critical line.

targets: Formalize finite Euler-product and explicit-formula toy statements.

questions: Can a spectral or cohomological avatar force critical-line symmetry?

references.label: Clay Mathematics Institute — Millennium Prize Problems

title: Yang-Mills existence and mass gap
summary: Construct quantum Yang-Mills theory on four-dimensional space-time and prove a positive mass gap.

targets: Model finite gauge systems and spectral gaps before continuum theory.

questions: Which finite or constructive model preserves the gap mechanism?

references.label: Clay Mathematics Institute — Millennium Prize Problems

title: Navier-Stokes existence and smoothness
summary: Determine global existence and smoothness, or finite-time breakdown, for 3D incompressible Navier-Stokes.

targets: Formalize a simplified energy inequality with explicit norms.

questions: Can critical-scale energy cascade be blocked by a hidden monotonicity?

references.label: Clay Mathematics Institute — Millennium Prize Problems

title: Hodge Conjecture
summary: Certain rational cohomology classes on smooth projective complex varieties should come from algebraic cycles.

targets: Represent toy algebraic cycles and cohomology classes as explicit data.

questions: Which cycle-detection problem survives in finite toy geometries?

references.label: Clay Mathematics Institute — Millennium Prize Problems

title: Birch and Swinnerton-Dyer Conjecture
summary: The rank of an elliptic curve over the rationals should equal the order of vanishing of its L-function at one.

targets: Map current elliptic curve formalization and finite-rank toy analogues.

questions: Can analytic rank be approximated by a formal finite or modular proxy?

references.label: Clay Mathematics Institute — Millennium Prize Problems

title: P vs NP
summary: Determine whether every efficiently verifiable problem is efficiently solvable.

targets: Formalize reductions and tiny circuit/proof systems.

questions: Which lower-bound barrier can be encoded as a robust invariant?

references.label: Clay Mathematics Institute — Millennium Prize Problems

title: Poincare Conjecture
summary: Every closed simply connected 3-manifold is homeomorphic to the 3-sphere; solved by Perelman.

targets: Use as a calibration case for topology, geometry and flow interactions.

questions: Can the graph recover why geometric flow was the decisive language?

references.label: Clay Mathematics Institute — Millennium Prize Problems
